proposals.name: Boring-on-Purpose: Vite 5 + React 18.3.1 + TypeScript strict, self-hosted vendors, gate-blocked Actions deploy
proposals.summary: Keep the exact runtime the pixels depend on (react-dom 18.3.1 + recharts 2.12.7, verbatim-ported JSX and inline styles) and change only the delivery mechanism: Vite 5 builds TypeScript ES modules into a static dist/ that GitHub Pages serves via an Actions artifact deploy that is physically impossible without the verify gate passing first — structurally closing R-GATE/C-26/B-01. Frozen crisis data moves out of dashboard.jsx into JSON files whose canonical hash is proven equal to the existing golden hash BEFORE the old file is deleted; pure math (interp/rebase/mulberry32/runFan/corr/buildAggregate) is ported line-for-line into small typed modules that the existing mutation harness and invariant tests retarget with minimal change. Bundling from a committed lockfile removes unpkg from the egress allowlist entirely (SRI's job is inherited by npm-ci integrity hashes + SHA-pinned actions + a double-build reproducibility check + SLSA provenance attestation). The bubblegauge stays behind the identical ?status-api contract as a lazily imported chunk whose activation predicate becomes a directly unit-testable pure function. A Playwright acceptance suite (DOM-number assertions, request-interception egress proof, screenshot tolerance) is frozen against the CURRENT site first and must pass unchanged against dist/ — that suite, not judgment, is the parity instrument.

NO upgrade, NO replacement: fan charts, the matrix heatmap, and ~15 custom tooltips are the single largest drift surface, and version-identity is the cheapest insurance available. Zero-footprint without the param is preserved and strictly improved: today the inert 74KB bubblegauge.jsx is always downloaded; after, nothing bubblegauge-related is fetched at all (the chunk fetch when active is same-origin static, not egress). proposals.structure: Repo root: index.html (Vite entry, ~30 lines: meta, favicon, noscript, cwFail error shim, one module script), vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, package.json + package-lock.json, public/CNAME (sole literal-domain file, DR-005 preserved) + public/.nojekyll. src/ in ~45 small files: src/main.tsx (mount + activation check + lazy import); src/app/ (App.tsx shell, TabBar.tsx, theme.ts); src/data/ — crises.json, matrix.json (+ mx-crises), classification.json, categories.json, classes.json, extracted mechanically from dashboard.jsx by a one-shot script that serializes the ALREADY-LOADED objects (same insertion order, same JSON.stringify) so the golden hash is provably unchanged, plus index.ts (typed loader: Crisis, SeriesAnchor = [month, value] tuple, MatrixCell = -1|0|1|2|3 union types — the type system now encodes what 10-data-invariants asserts); src/lib/ — interp.ts, rebase.ts, rng.ts (mulberry32), fan.ts (runFan, seed=7/11, sims=1500, block=6, H=60 as named constants), corr.ts (zArr/corrArr/xcorrRow), aggregate.ts, format.ts — each a verbatim function-body copy, one export per concern, all pure, no React import allowed (enforced by an eslint boundary rule + the egress/static test); src/tabs/ — one directory per tab (CrisisExplorer, SimilarityMatrix, Aggregate, Analytics, Playbook), components split at ~250-line granularity; src/bubblegauge/ — contract.ts, activation.ts, api.ts (fetch + AbortController), fixtures.ts (demo/fixture payloads), components/, index.ts lazy entry. tests/acceptance/*.spec.ts (Playwright, FROZEN). verify/ stays the gate (paths updated), audit/ + governance/ stay. Old dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx are deleted only in the final migration commit, after the hash-equality and acceptance proofs are recorded in a decision record.
proposals.deploy: Switch Pages Source to "GitHub Actions". Two workflows, all third-party actions pinned by commit SHA: (1) verify.yml unchanged in role — run.js + mutation.js + gate.js on every push/PR, plus now `npm ci && vitest run && npx playwright test` and `npm run build` with post-build checks; made a required status check via ruleset on main (flip the ruleset to enforcing — the standing R-GATE gap). (2) deploy.yml on push to main: job A `gate` = full verify suite (harness + unit + mutation + acceptance against the built dist via `vite preview`); job B `build` (needs: gate) = `npm ci && vite build`, then post-build verification IN the workflow: egress scan over dist/** (allowlist now excludes unpkg), dist file inventory vs expectation, double-build determinism check (build twice into separate dirs, `diff -r` — reproducibility made executable); job C `attest+deploy` (needs: build, permissions: id-token:write, pages:write, attestations:write, environment: github-pages) = actions/attest-build-provenance@v2 over the artifact (SLSA provenance binding source commit -> served bytes, upgrading the honest R-LEDGER residual), actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3, actions/deploy-pages@v4. Net effect: a red gate cannot deploy — R-GATE, C-26 (pins), and B-01 close STRUCTURALLY, not procedurally; git push alone no longer publishes. CLAUDE.md zero-build section amended by decision record (owner has opened that door); rollback story: Pages can be flipped back to branch-deploy of a kept last-known-good tag if Actions is ever unavailable.

proposals.testing: Three layers. (1) FROZEN acceptance suite, written FIRST against the current zero-build site (constraint 6): Playwright 1.48.x, black-box over http://localhost:8000 — all 5 tabs render with exact displayed numbers asserted from the DOM (med36 62/132, matrix cell values, aggregate endpoints — the mutation oracle's golden numbers become user-visible assertions); fan-chart SVG path sampling (first/mid/last point coordinates within tolerance); toHaveScreenshot per tab with maxDiffPixelRatio 0.01 (catches Recharts drift without flaking on AA); the full ?status-api contract as behavior: no param => no strip/6th tab AND zero non-allowlisted requests (request interception), =demo => strip + 6th tab + fixture values offline, bad key rejected by KEY_RE, sessionStorage persistence across reload, ?status-api-off clears, localhost mapping. This suite runs green on the OLD site in CI before any rewrite commit, then must pass unchanged against `vite preview` of dist/. (2) Unit: Vitest for src/lib (all existing invariant + golden-value assertions ported) and src/bubblegauge/activation.ts (every gating branch table-tested — the contract's most security-sensitive logic finally gets direct coverage). (3) Standing gate: verify/run.js + mutation.js + gate.js as today, wired as required checks and as the deploy precondition. tsc --noEmit and an eslint import-boundary rule (lib may not import react; tabs may not import bubblegauge statically) run in the same job — cheap agent-maintainability wins under no-human-review.
proposals.migration: Strictly ordered, each step lands only with the gate green: (0) Decision record amending CLAUDE.md's zero-build constraint (owner-authorized) + this architecture. (1) Write and freeze the acceptance suite against the CURRENT site; add it to CI; record its hash in the ratchet (suite may not be weakened post-freeze without a decision record). (2) Scaffold Vite+TS at root alongside the old files (old site untouched and still served); commit exact-pinned package.json + lockfile. (3) Data extraction: script serializes CRISES/MATRIX/MX_CRISES/CLASSIFICATION/CAT/CLS from the existing loader into src/data/*.json; CI proves triple hash equality (old loader, new JSON, committed golden) — this is the no-silent-number-change proof. (4) Port pure math verbatim to src/lib; port invariant tests to Vitest; retarget mutation.js; re-run calibration corpus to 9/9. (5) Port dashboard tabs one at a time, verbatim JSX->TSX; after each, acceptance runs against `vite build && vite preview`. (6) Port bubblegauge: activation.ts + unit table, lazy chunk, api/fixtures; acceptance demo-mode specs pass. (7) Retarget verify/tests 30/35/61/62/63/70 to the new layout; regenerate provenance manifest; gate self-test (50) re-proves each control can still fail. (8) Cutover commit: replace old index.html/dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx, flip Pages to Actions deploy with the gate-blocked deploy.yml, make the ruleset enforcing, update engagement-status/findings (close R-GATE, C-26, B-01 via decision records). (9) Remove unpkg from allowlists and pins manifest; final audit-doc sweep. Estimated agent effort: the port (5-6) is mechanical; the real work is steps 1, 3, 7 — exactly where the evaluation says the risk lives.
proposals.risks: [{"risk": "Chart drift: Recharts ESM build (tree-shaken, different module graph) renders subtly differently than the UMD bundle despite identical version — e.g. defaultProps resolution order or ResponsiveContainer measurement timing shifting tooltip/label positions.", "mitigation": "Exact 2.12.7 pin (same published source the UMD wraps); per-tab screenshot comparisons against baselines captured from the LIVE UMD site before migration (tolerance 1%); SVG path-coordinate sampling assertions independent of raster; per-tab migration so any drift is bisected to one component; recharts upgrade explicitly out of scope."}, {"risk": "Number drift in the seeded deterministic pipeline (mulberry32 + runFan sims=1500): any 'cleanup' during the TS port (operator order, |0 vs Math.floor, block bootstrap indexing) silently changes displayed medians/drawdowns.", "mitigation": "Verbatim function-body copies (port rule: change imports/exports only, never expressions); the existing golden interior values (interp[13]=124.9167, med36 62/132, mdd -0.6307, aggregate endpoints) asserted in unit tests AND in DOM by the frozen acceptance suite; mutation harness re-proves the assertions still kill fault classes on the new modules."}, {"risk": "Golden-hash breakage during JSON extraction — key insertion order, -0, 1e-9 notation, or a trailing float digit changes JSON.stringify output and the hash goes red for a non-change (or worse, goes green over a real change because someone 'fixed' the baseline).", "mitigation": "Extraction serializes the objects the existing verify/lib/load.js ALREADY produces (identity transform, not re-typing); CI step asserts hash(old loader) === hash(new JSON) === committed golden before the old file can be deleted; any mismatch is a hard stop, and baseline updates remain decision-record-gated per Article/ratchet rules."}, {"risk": "The 119-check audit apparatus is regex-coupled to the current 3-file layout; retargeting 11 verify tests + gate + calibration seeds could quietly turn a control vacuous (passes because it no longer matches anything).", "mitigation": "Controls migrate one at a time with the gate self-test (50-gate-selftest) extended per control: each retargeted check must be demonstrated to FAIL on a seeded violation in the new layout before its green counts; calibration corpus re-run to 9/9 catch rate (ratcheted, cannot regress); test 35's stray-suite tripwire keeps covering the new tests/ tree."}, {"risk": "The Actions deploy pipeline becomes the new supply-chain/SPOF surface: a compromised action, a lockfile-poisoned dev dep, or Actions downtime now sits between a green gate and the served site — a class of risk the zero-build design didn't have.", "mitigation": "All actions pinned by commit SHA; job permissions minimal (id-token/pages/attestations only in the deploy job, contents:read elsewhere); npm ci from the committed lockfile with registry-only resolved URLs (lockfile-lint test); double-build determinism diff makes the transform verifiable; SLSA attestation binds artifact to commit; documented rollback = flip Pages back to branch-deploy of a tagged last-known-good dist committed to a gh-pages-fallback branch."}]
proposals.tradeoffs: What this buys: gate becomes deploy-blocking (the single biggest open governance blocker closes structurally), ~3MB Babel + in-browser transpile of 3000 lines on every load disappears (first paint drops from multi-second transpile to a ~200KB-gzip vendor+app load with the bubblegauge code-split out), unpkg egress is eliminated, pure math and the gating contract become typed, small, directly unit-testable modules — the properties agent-maintainability under no-human-review actually needs. What it costs, honestly: a build step and lockfile the agents must keep green forever (~10 dev deps: vite, plugin-react, typescript, vitest, playwright, eslint + boundary plugin, lockfile-lint — a real, permanent maintenance surface); npm-ecosystem exposure at build time replacing CDN exposure at load time (smaller but not zero); roughly 7 of 11 verify tests need retargeting and the calibration corpus needs re-seeding — budgeted as first-class migration work, not cleanup; CLAUDE.md's ratified zero-build framing needs a decision record the owner has already signaled. Deliberately NOT done, and why: no React 19/Recharts 3 (pure parity risk, zero requirement pull), no router/SSR/SSG framework (one page; Vite MPA-less SPA is the minimum machine), no Tailwind/CSS-in-JS migration (styling is frozen look, churn without payoff), no Stryker (bespoke deterministic mutator already calibrated), no hand-rolled SVG chart rewrite (months of parity risk to remove one well-pinned dependency). This is the boring option because every exotic alternative trades parity certainty — the top-weighted criterion — for novelty this repo's governance regime would then have to re-audit.

proposals.name: Modernized Zero-Build: Native ESM + htm, Self-Hosted Vendors, Actions-Gated Deploy
proposals.summary: Keep the repo == the site (no bundler, no transform between git and Pages), but replace in-browser Babel transpilation of two monolithic JSX files with ~30 small native ES modules using htm tagged templates bound to React.createElement. React/ReactDOM/PropTypes/Recharts stay at today's EXACT pinned UMD bytes, but self-hosted in vendor/ (unpkg egress removed entirely); a 4-entry import map plus tiny shim modules bridge the UMD globals into the module graph. Recharts is honestly kept as UMD because recharts@2.12.7 ESM is un-servable without a bundler (bare lodash/* and victory-vendor/* subpath imports) — this yields zero chart-library drift risk. The optional bubblegauge becomes a dynamically-imported module subtree (true zero-footprint AND free code-splitting without a bundler). @babel/standalone (~3 MB + 1-3 s main-thread transpile per load) disappears, enabling a real CSP. Types come from JSDoc + CI-only `tsc --noEmit --checkJs` (no build step). Pages deploy flips to "GitHub Actions" source with the verify gate + frozen Playwright acceptance as deploy-blocking steps and artifact attestation — structurally closing R-GATE/C-26/B-01. Because there is no build, the deployed artifact is byte-identical to the audited commit: the reproducible-build problem is solved by construction.
proposals.stack: {"language": "Modern JavaScript (ES2022 native modules) with JSDoc type annotations; NO TypeScript syntax in served files — typescript@5.5.4 runs CI-only as `tsc --noEmit --allowJs --checkJs --strict` against served .js + ambient types/*.d.ts (window.Recharts, BubbleGauge interface). No transpilation ever.", "framework": "React 18.3.1 — the SAME react/react-dom/prop-types UMD production bytes as today, self-hosted in vendor/ with recorded upstream hashes. preact/compat explicitly rejected: recharts + react-smooth rely on findDOMNode/ref semantics where compat shims are a known drift source; gratuitous parity risk for ~35 KB saved.", "templating": "htm@3.1.1 (self-hosted single-file ESM, ~700 B, zero deps) bound once: `export const html = htm.bind(React.createElement)`. JSX is eliminated; htm syntax is near-1:1 with JSX (`<${Tab} active=${x}>`), so the port is mechanical and diffs stay reviewable-by-test.", "charts": "recharts@2.12.7 UMD kept byte-exact and self-hosted (vendor/Recharts.js). HONEST ASSESSMENT: recharts 2.x ESM cannot run bundler-less in a browser — its es6 build imports bare CJS-pathed specifiers (lodash/isFunction et al., victory-vendor/d3-*, react-smooth, recharts-scale, eventemitter3, react-is, clsx), each with transitive bare imports; an import map covering that graph would need hundreds of entries and lodash has no per-method browser-ESM files. Rebuilt ESM from esm.sh/skypack is a supply-chain regression. UMD-as-classic-script with a shim module (`vendor/shims/recharts.js` re-exporting the named exports actually used, with a fail-fast existence assertion) is the only zero-drift, zero-build option. Hand-rolled SVG rejected: fan charts + custom tooltips + matrix are exactly where hand-rolling maximizes parity risk. No upgrade to Recharts 3.x (breaking).", "module_loading": "index.html loads 4 classic vendor scripts (SRI, same-origin) in today's order, then one `<script type='module' src='./app/main.js'>`. A single import map maps exactly 4 bare specifiers (react, react-dom/client, prop-types, recharts → vendor/shims/*.js; htm → vendor/htm.module.js). All app imports are relative paths — if an older browser lacks import-map support (Safari <16.4, ~2023 baseline), degrading to relative shim imports is a pure textual change.", "styling": "Unchanged: inline style objects (parity-exact port), shared tokens extracted to app/theme.js. No CSS frameworks, no CSS files beyond the existing <style> reset.", "runtime_deps_shipped": "5 vendored files total (react, react-dom, prop-types, Recharts, htm). Zero npm dependencies of shipped code; zero runtime CDN egress.", "ci_deps": "verify/ harness keeps node:test-style custom runner; adds typescript@5.5.4 (checker only), playwright@1.45.1 (acceptance), keeps existing esbuild only until dashboard.jsx is retired, then deletes it — native ESM imports directly into Node, removing the verify/lib/load.js eval-shim indirection entirely."}
proposals.structure: Repo root stays the served root (Pages artifact = repo subset). Layout: index.html (import map + 5 script tags + existing error/noscript shell); vendor/ {react.production.min.js, react-dom.production.min.js, prop-types.min.js, Recharts.js, htm.module.js, shims/{react.js, react-dom-client.js, prop-types.js, recharts.js}}; data/ {crises.js, matrix.js, classification.js, categories.js} — frozen crisis data as ESM modules exporting deep-frozen plain objects (data-as-data; .js not .json because JSON import attributes are still patchy in Safari and fetch() would break the synchronous render path); app/main.js (bootstrap: render shell, run gating, dynamic-import bubblegauge); app/lib/ {rng.js (mulberry32), fan.js (runFan, sims=1500, block=6, H=60 — byte-identical port), stats.js, format.js} — pure, DOM-free, directly importable in Node; app/tabs/ {explorer.js, matrix.js, aggregate.js, analytics.js, playbook.js}; app/charts/ {fan-chart.js, matrix-grid.js, tooltips.js} (shared recharts wrappers); app/bubblegauge/ {gating.js (KEY_RE, sessionStorage, ?status-api-off, localhost mapping — near-verbatim port, statically imported ~1 KB), index.js, feed.js, gauge.js, fixtures.js} — everything except gating.js loads ONLY via `await import()` after a key resolves, so the without-param page fetches zero bubblegauge bytes (stronger than today's 'inert but loaded'); types/ {globals.d.ts, bubblegauge.d.ts}. The window.BubbleGauge global bridge dies: main.js passes the resolved BubbleGauge interface ({enabled, Strip, ExtraTab, metrics, reanchor}) into the dashboard shell as an explicit prop, typed in bubblegauge.d.ts. ~30 files, largest tab module ~350 lines — sized for agent-context refactor safety.
proposals.deploy: Pages source flips from 'deploy from branch' to 'GitHub Actions' (DR + CLAUDE.md amendment). Workflow on push to main: (1) checkout; (2) verify gate — `cd verify && npm ci && node run.js && node gate.js`; (3) `tsc --noEmit` type gate; (4) serve repo via `python3 -m http.server`, run frozen Playwright acceptance (chromium + webkit); (5) actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with an explicit served-file allowlist (index.html, CNAME, .nojekyll, vendor/, data/, app/) — verify/, audit/, governance/, acceptance/ are no longer even published; (6) actions/deploy-pages@v4; (7) actions/attest-build-provenance@v2 on the artifact tarball. Because zero-build means artifact bytes == commit tree subset, provenance is trivial and a verify test recomputes the artifact manifest hash deterministically. This makes the gate DEPLOY-BLOCKING (a red gate means no deploy, regardless of ruleset enforcement) — the structural close of R-GATE, C-26 and B-01 that branch-push deploys cannot provide. Rollback = re-run deploy from a prior green commit.
proposals.controls_mapping: {"golden_data_hash": "verify/tests/20-golden-content retargets from `loadDashboard()` (esbuild-eval of dashboard.jsx) to `import('../../data/*.js')` in Node — same payload keys (CRISES, MATRIX, MX_CRISES, CLASSIFICATION, CAT, CLS), same sha256; because content is identical the recorded hash in verify/golden/data-hash.json should NOT change (a changed hash during migration is itself a red flag). One DR for the loader mechanics.", "static_security": "verify/tests/30 + 62 regexes retarget to app/**/*.js and vendor/shims/; ADD: a real CSP via <meta http-equiv> — `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:` — impossible today because text/babel requires eval. connect-src stays open-https (the prod status-API host may not be written into the repo per privacy rule; note this limitation in the DR). New rule: `window.Recharts`/`window.React` referenced ONLY inside vendor/shims/ (regex-enforced).", "sri_and_pins": "35-supply-chain retargets from unpkg-URL matching to verify/golden/vendor-pins.json: {file, package, version, upstream_url, sha384} per vendor/ file; test recomputes sha384 of local bytes — the previously online-only 40-sri-recompute becomes FULLY OFFLINE-DETERMINISTIC (no more NO-EVIDENCE-OFFLINE fallback); an optional weekly advisory workflow cross-checks local bytes against unpkg. index.html keeps integrity attributes on the classic vendor scripts (harmless same-origin defense-in-depth).", "provenance_manifest": "governance/provenance-manifest.json extends to every vendor/ file (upstream URL, retrieval date, sha384, 'byte-exact upstream copy' assertion) and every app/ module; 61-provenance test recomputes. The Actions artifact attestation adds deploy-time provenance on top.", "egress_allowlist": "62-security-surface shrinks its allowlist: unpkg removed entirely (preconnect deleted), leaving only the gated status-API pattern + etoro outbound anchors; a Playwright acceptance test asserts via route interception that a paramless load performs ZERO cross-origin requests — a strict improvement over today.", "mutation_testing": "verify/mutation.js floor 0.75 retargets to app/lib/*.js and app/bubblegauge/gating.js imported natively — no esbuild strip, no stub-global eval; mutants are plain module-text mutations run under node:test. Coverage honesty improves because the tested artifact IS the shipped artifact.", "calibration_seeds": "The 9-seed defect corpus (BG_REPO pointing at seeded copies) carries over; each RETARGETED test must first demonstrate it still catches its corpus defect in the new layout before the migration DR closes — this is the guard against silent control-weakening during the port. acceptance/golden/computed-fans.json + computed-aggregate.json re-verify by importing app/lib/fan.js in Node and asserting byte-identical mulberry32/runFan output.", "gate_selftest_governance_claims": "50/60/61/70 need path retargets only; gate.js fail-closed production-eligibility logic unchanged; constitution digest hash-binding untouched.", "feargreed_contract": "63-feargreed-contract retargets to app/bubblegauge/feed.js; browser still never calls CNN (contract unchanged, now also CSP-visible)."}
proposals.testing: Two frozen layers before any code moves. (1) Acceptance (black-box, frozen pre-migration, must pass unchanged after): playwright@1.45.1, chromium+webkit, against `python3 -m http.server` — all 5 tabs render; displayed numbers extracted from DOM and compared to acceptance/golden/atlas-data + computed-fans/computed-aggregate; per-tab screenshot goldens (maxDiffPixelRatio 0.001); full ?status-api contract: demo mode renders fixtures offline, invalid keys rejected by KEY_RE, sessionStorage persistence across navigation, ?status-api-off clears, localhost mapping, and paramless load shows no strip/no 6th tab/zero cross-origin requests (route interception). (2) Unit (new, cheap): node:test over app/lib/ (mulberry32 sequences, runFan seed=fixed sims=1500 golden vectors, stats/format edge cases) and app/bubblegauge/gating.js (KEY_RE table, storage fallbacks) — these are the mutation-testing substrate. (3) Migration-only scaffolding (deleted after): a jsdom + react-dom/server harness rendering old-JSX (esbuild-stripped, the existing verify/lib/load.js machinery) vs new-htm modules to renderToStaticMarkup and diffing HTML per tab — catches htm conversion drift (boolean attrs, whitespace text nodes, key placement) deterministically rather than pixel-statistically.
proposals.migration: Seven gated steps, each landing green with a DR: (1) FREEZE — extend acceptance/ to the full contract above, record goldens against the current site; tag the commit. (2) VENDOR — copy the 5 unpkg files byte-exact into vendor/ (+htm), verify sha384 against current SRI values, point index.html at ./vendor/, delete preconnect; retarget 35/40; behavior identical, egress win banked immediately. (3) DEPLOY FLIP — Pages source → Actions, gate+acceptance deploy-blocking, artifact attestation; closes R-GATE/C-26/B-01 while the code is still the old code (deliberately decoupled from the rewrite). (4) BUBBLEGAUGE FIRST — port bubblegauge.jsx → app/bubblegauge/ ESM+htm, dynamic import from a new tiny main.js; keep writing window.BubbleGauge so the still-babel dashboard.jsx reads it unchanged (temporary bridge); smaller file, optional feature, fixture-testable = lowest-risk rehearsal of the whole method. (5) DASHBOARD — extract data/ modules (golden hash must NOT change), then port the shell + tabs one module per change, old-vs-new HTML-diff harness green at each step; babel tag remains until the last tab lands. (6) CUT BABEL — remove @babel/standalone and text/babel tags, delete window.BubbleGauge bridge (prop injection instead), add CSP meta, add tsc gate; retarget mutation + calibration to native imports; delete verify's esbuild shim. (7) AUDIT SWEEP — re-run the 119-check catalogue mapping, re-demonstrate each retargeted control against its calibration seed, amend CLAUDE.md architecture section, update residual-risk register (R-VENDOR improves: vendored bytes + offline hash check; R-GATE closes).
proposals.risks: [{"risk": "htm conversion drift — subtle JSX/htm semantic differences (boolean attribute handling, whitespace text nodes, spread props, component-close `<//>`) silently change rendered output across 3,000 lines.", "mitigation": "Three independent nets: frozen per-tab Playwright screenshot + DOM-number goldens; a migration-only renderToStaticMarkup old-vs-new HTML diff harness (deterministic, catches sub-pixel-invisible DOM changes); one-tab-per-gated-change so any drift bisects to ~300 lines. Bubblegauge ports first as a rehearsal."}, {"risk": "Recharts UMD global-bridge fragility, or a future agent 'helpfully' swapping to a rebuilt ESM CDN copy or Recharts 3.x — the single biggest parity and supply-chain hazard.", "mitigation": "Decision record pins recharts@2.12.7 UMD bytes-forever (hash in vendor-pins.json, offline-recomputed every gate run); vendor/shims/recharts.js asserts every named export used and fails fast; static-security regex bans window.Recharts outside the shim; CLAUDE.md amendment states the ESM-recharts-without-a-bundler impossibility explicitly so it isn't relitigated."}, {"risk": "The 119-check verify apparatus is regex-coupled to the current layout — retargeting ~11 test files could silently weaken a control (a test that passes because it now matches nothing).", "mitigation": "Reuse the calibration corpus as the acceptance bar for the migration itself: every retargeted control must first RE-CATCH its seeded defect in the new layout before its DR closes; gate self-test (50) extended to assert non-vacuity (each suite executed >0 assertions against >0 matched files); one control per change, never batched with feature moves."}, {"risk": "No bundler means no minification/tree-shaking: Recharts.js UMD ships unminified (~4-5 MB raw, ~600-800 KB gzipped via Pages), and ~30 module files add request waterfall latency.", "mitigation": "Net UX is still a large win — Babel's ~3 MB + 1-3 s main-thread transpile disappears and vendors cache immutably; modulepreload hints flatten the app-module waterfall; if size proves painful, a DR'd one-time vendoring transform (recorded terser@5.31.x invocation, input+output hashes in the provenance manifest — a documented vendoring step, not a per-deploy build) minifies the committed vendor copy."}, {"risk": "?status-api contract regression — gating is the subtlest behavior (KEY_RE, sessionStorage persistence, ?status-api-off, localhost mapping, zero-footprint paramless load), and the bridge changes from a load-order global to prop injection.", "mitigation": "gating.js is a near-verbatim line-for-line port, unit-tested against a KEY_RE/storage truth table AND mutation-tested; the frozen acceptance layer covers every clause of INTEGRATION_NOTES.md including a zero-cross-origin-requests assertion; the window.BubbleGauge bridge is retired only in step 6, after both sides are module-native and acceptance has passed with the bridge in place."}]
proposals.tradeoffs: What this buys: near-zero chart/framework drift risk (same React and Recharts bytes as production today), the smallest possible CI surface for eternal agent maintenance (no bundler config, no lockfile for shipped code, deploy artifact == commit), unpkg egress eliminated, a real CSP, code-split bubblegauge via native dynamic import, and verify controls that mostly get STRONGER (offline SRI, native-import mutation testing). What it costs, honestly: no TypeScript syntax (JSDoc+tsc is ~85% of the value with real friction on complex generics); htm is a niche dialect agents know less well than JSX (mitigated by its near-JSX shape and the type checker); no minification/tree-shaking so payload is bigger than a Vite build would produce (~700 KB gz vendors vs ~250 KB bundled — but far below today's effective cost, and with zero per-load CPU tax); the UMD-globals shim is an acknowledged wart carried so that chart parity is guaranteed rather than hoped for; and the browser-support floor rises to import-map-era browsers (Safari 16.4+/Chrome 89+... effectively 2023+), which is acceptable for a research dashboard. This proposal deliberately spends its risk budget on the ONE unavoidable transformation (JSX → htm) and refuses every optional transformation (framework swap, chart-lib swap, bundler adoption) — the correct shape when 25% of the grade is parity and 25% is keeping a no-human-review gate green forever.

proposals.name: Astro Static Shell + Typed React Islands ("island-fortress")
proposals.summary: Astro 5 in pure static-output mode produces a pre-rendered HTML shell (header, tab chrome, the prose-heavy Playbook content, footer, noscript) at build time, while the interactive atlas hydrates as ONE React 18.3.1 island and the bubblegauge is a SECOND, code-split island whose JS chunk is never even fetched unless ?status-api matches KEY_RE. All vendor code (react, react-dom, recharts@2.12.7 exact-pinned) is bundled and self-hosted, eliminating unpkg egress entirely. Frozen crisis data moves out of JSX into canonical JSON under src/data/ (golden hash now computed over data files directly, not parsed source), pure math moves into small TypeScript modules with exact-value unit tests, and deployment flips to GitHub Pages 'deploy from Actions' where verify/'s gate is a hard job dependency of the deploy job — structurally closing R-GATE/C-26/B-01 and adding actions/attest-build-provenance on the exact artifact served. Honest caveat baked into the design: this page is app-heavy, not content-heavy, so Astro is chosen for its enforced island boundary, zero-config Vite/TS toolchain, and free code-splitting of the optional module — not for MDX content pipelines, which we deliberately do not use.
proposals.stack: {"framework": "Astro 5.x (output:'static', no SSR adapter) + @astrojs/react integration; React and react-dom pinned EXACTLY 18.3.1 (same version as today's UMD — minimizes reconciler/behavior drift)", "language": "TypeScript 5.x strict for all new code (src/lib, src/components, src/islands); data as plain JSON validated by zod 3.x schemas at build+test time (zod is dev/build-side only; nothing schema-related ships to the browser beyond what the compiler emits)", "build": "Astro's built-in Vite (whatever Vite 6.x Astro 5 pins), Node 20 LTS pinned in CI via .nvmrc + setup-node, npm ci against a committed package-lock.json; all versions exact-pinned (save-exact=true in .npmrc), zero floating ranges", "charts": "KEEP recharts@2.12.7 exact-pinned, installed from npm and bundled (identical library version to today's UMD build = same rendering code paths for the fan charts' stacked Areas, the custom tooltips, and the div-grid similarity matrix). NO upgrade to Recharts 3.x (breaking), NO replacement, NO hand-rolled SVG — the 25% parity-risk criterion dominates and version-identical self-hosting is the lowest-drift option available. Revisit an upgrade only post-migration, behind the frozen acceptance suite", "styling": "Port the existing inline React style objects VERBATIM (dashboard.jsx has ~277 inline style sites — they are already React style objects, so they compile unchanged); no Tailwind, no CSS framework, no global reset changes (a reset would shift ResponsiveContainer measurements). Optional later refactor to colocated CSS Modules per component, gated by the screenshot suite", "bubblegauge_boundary": "Module boundary replaces the window.BubbleGauge global bridge: src/islands/bubblegauge/ exports a typed interface { enabled, key, base, demo, StatusStrip, StatusTab, useFeed } consumed by the dashboard island. A ~1 KB inline gate script in the Astro shell re-implements the exact current key resolution (KEY_RE /^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$/, ?status-api-off sessionStorage clear, sessionStorage persistence, localhost→http://localhost:8000 mapping, demo/fixture mode) and only then does the dashboard island lazy-load the bubblegauge chunk via dynamic import(). Zero-footprint without the param is now enforceable at the network layer: the chunk file is simply never requested (today the inert 74 KB bubblegauge.jsx is always downloaded and transpiled)", "dependencies_shipped": "runtime deps: react, react-dom, recharts (+ its transitive d3-* / victory-vendor tree — acknowledged in SBOM). dev deps: astro, @astrojs/react, typescript, zod, vitest, playwright. A dependency-count ratchet test makes any addition require a decision record"}
proposals.structure: src/data/ — crises.json, matrix.json (+ mx-crises.json), classification.json, cat.json, cls.json: byte-for-byte the values currently in dashboard.jsx lines 16–302, extracted mechanically and proven equal by a one-shot equivalence script (evaluate old JSX constants in Node via esbuild — verify/ already vendors esbuild — deep-equal against the new JSON) before the golden hash is re-baselined under a decision record. src/lib/ — pure math, one concern per file, each <150 lines: rng.ts (mulberry32), interp.ts, rebase.ts, format.ts (fmtM etc.), zscore.ts, similarity.ts (rolling-K z-correlation windows), fan.ts (block-bootstrap fan simulation, seed+sims=1500 as named exported constants), aggregate.ts (class bucketing), quantile.ts. These are the mutation-testing and unit-test targets and are import-shared with verify/ and acceptance/ so the gate exercises the SAME code the browser runs (today verify re-evaluates JSX to approximate this). src/components/ — per-tab folders: explorer/, matrix/, aggregate/, analytics/, playbook/, plus shared/ (tooltips, legends, cards, tab bar); target 25–35 files, none >300 lines. src/islands/Dashboard.tsx — the single main island mounted client:only=\"react\" (the app is one stateful SPA with cross-tab state; splitting tabs into separate islands would fragment shared state for no win — this is the honest answer to 'is it content-heavy enough': mostly no). src/islands/bubblegauge/ — the lazy second island as above. src/pages/index.astro — the only page; server-renders the static shell: title, disclaimer, tab chrome skeleton, the Playbook tab's editorial prose as real HTML (the one genuinely content-shaped part — meaningful first paint + works in noscript readers), then mounts the island. public/ — .nojekyll, CNAME, demo fixture JSON. verify/ stays at repo root (retargeted, see controls). acceptance/ stays at repo root (frozen). No router (tab state stays in-component as today), no analytics, no service worker — unchanged posture.
proposals.deploy: Switch Pages source from 'deploy from branch' to 'GitHub Actions'. Single workflow, four jobs with hard needs: edges — (1) gate: cd verify && npm ci && node run.js && node mutation.js && node gate.js (fail-closed, unchanged entry points); (2) build: npm ci && astro build, then build it a SECOND time in a clean dir and diff the two dist/ trees byte-for-byte (reproducibility check; SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH pinned to the commit timestamp), then regenerate governance/provenance-manifest.json entries for every dist/ file and run verify/tests/61 against dist/; (3) attest: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2 over the Pages artifact (signed SLSA provenance — upgrades the manifest's honest R-LEDGER residual from 'git-anchored substitute' to cryptographic attestation, exactly the 'buildable upgrade' the manifest itself names); (4) deploy: actions/configure-pages@v5 + actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 + actions/deploy-pages@v4, needs:[gate,build,attest], main-branch only, environment: github-pages. Because the ONLY path to production is now this workflow, the gate is structurally deploy-blocking regardless of ruleset enforcement — a push to main with a red gate simply never becomes a deployment. This closes R-GATE and B-01 and converts C-26 from PARTIAL toward CLOSED. CNAME handling: keep the CNAME file copied into dist/ (the sole place the production domain appears, per DR-005). CLAUDE.md's zero-build clause and the constitution are amended by decision records BEFORE the cutover commit (the owner has explicitly opened this door; Article XIV is satisfied because the invariant is amended through the sanctioned DR process, not breached).
proposals.controls_mapping: {"golden_data_hash": "STRENGTHENED. verify/tests/20 stops evaluating JSX and hashes the canonical JSON files in src/data/ directly (sha256 over the same payload shape {CRISES, MATRIX, MX_CRISES, CLASSIFICATION, CAT, CLS}). One intentional re-baseline of verify/golden/data-hash.json, filed as a decision record whose evidence is the mechanical extraction equivalence proof (old-constants-deep-equal-new-JSON script output committed to audit/decisions/). Friction preserved: editing any data file still flips the hash and still demands a DR", "static_security": "RETARGETED, two layers. Source layer: the regex checks in verify/tests/30 (KEY_RE-enforced, no injection sinks, no fabricated success, hardened links) move from dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx to src/**/*.{ts,tsx} and src/pages/index.astro. Artifact layer (new, stronger): the same sink/URL scans additionally run over dist/ output in CI, catching anything a transform could introduce. Each retargeted check must re-pass its calibration seed (see mutation/calibration) so retargeting cannot leave a check vacuously green", "sri_and_pins": "TRANSFORMED. CDN SRI becomes moot — there are zero cross-origin scripts. Replacement controls: (a) committed package-lock.json + npm ci (integrity hashes for every package — SRI's moral equivalent at install time); (b) verify/tests/35 rewritten to assert exact pins from package.json/lockfile (react===18.3.1, recharts===2.12.7, no ^ or ~ anywhere) and a dependency-count ratchet (any NEW package name fails until a DR is filed, mirroring verify/golden/pinned-deps.json semantics); (c) governance/sbom.json regenerated from the lockfile including recharts' transitive d3/victory-vendor tree, honestly enlarging the declared supply-chain surface", "provenance_manifest": "UPGRADED. governance/provenance-manifest.json is regenerated at build over every dist/ file (hash, deriving source modules, owning role carried from the per-module mapping); verify/tests/61 verifies completeness + hash match against dist/ instead of repo-root files. Additionally the deployed Pages artifact gets a SIGNED attestation (actions/attest-build-provenance), closing the manifest's self-declared R-LEDGER gap", "egress_allowlist": "SHRUNK. verify/tests/62 retargets its URL-literal scan to src/** AND dist/** ; allowlist drops unpkg entirely and keeps only the gated status-API base derivation, the etoro outbound links, and localhost dev mapping; the no-direct-CNN-call check (tests/63) is unchanged in substance, now asserted against both source and bundle. Acceptance adds a Playwright network-interception test: load without ?status-api → assert ZERO non-same-origin requests (a stronger, behavioral form of the current static promise)", "mutation_testing": "IMPROVED. Keep the bespoke deterministic verify/mutation.js (no Stryker — no new dep, no nondeterminism) but point its mutants at src/lib/*.ts compiled via the already-vendored esbuild; the oracle becomes the Vitest exact-value unit tests plus the golden computed fixtures (acceptance/golden/computed-fans.json, computed-aggregate.json). Floor stays >=0.75 (current score is 1.00 on 13 mutants); the module split makes new mutants cheap to add per function, so grow the mutant set with the module count", "calibration_corpus": "PRESERVED AND EXTENDED. verify/calibration/corpus.json seeds get their file paths and injected-violation patches rewritten for the new layout; the rule stays: every gate check must demonstrably go RED on its seeded violation after retargeting. Add new seeds for the new failure modes this architecture introduces: a lockfile drift seed, a dist-nondeterminism seed, and a bubblegauge-chunk-loads-without-param seed", "gate_selftest_and_governance": "verify/tests/50 (gate self-test) and 60 (constitution hash binding, engagement-status integrity) carry over unchanged in mechanism; constitution digest hash updates flow through the same DR process as the zero-build amendment. gate.js's fail-closed production_eligible computation is untouched — this migration is precisely the work that lets R-GATE/C-26/B-01 be closed on evidence rather than argued away", "acceptance_suite": "FROZEN FIRST (constraint 6): black-box Playwright suite + numeric goldens recorded against the CURRENT site served by python3 -m http.server; after migration the identical suite runs against `astro preview` over dist/. The existing acceptance/golden/atlas-data.canonical.json and computed-* fixtures become the cross-architecture bridge: the new src/lib functions must reproduce them to full float precision"}
proposals.testing: Unit: Vitest 3.x (uses the same Vite pipeline as the build — no separate transform config to drift) over src/lib: exact-value assertions for mulberry32 sequences (first 100 draws for the shipped seed), interp/rebase/similarity outputs against acceptance/golden fixtures at full precision (no toBeCloseTo for frozen numbers — Number.prototype math is spec-deterministic, so verbatim-ported expressions must match bit-for-bit), zod schema validation of all src/data JSON, and the data invariants currently in verify/tests/10. Component: a thin layer of Vitest+@testing-library/react smoke tests for tab switching and tooltip formatting (kept small — the real oracle is acceptance). Acceptance (the frozen contract): Playwright 1.4x, ~40 specs: per-tab DOM assertions (headings, series counts, legend labels, specific formatted numbers lifted from computed goldens), fan-chart median values read from rendered SVG/tooltip text, similarity matrix cell values, full ?status-api contract matrix (valid key → strip+6th tab; invalid key → no-op; demo → fixture data, no network; status-api-off → sessionStorage cleared; reload persistence; localhost base mapping), zero-egress network interception without the param, and per-tab screenshot diffs at fixed viewport with a small maxDiffPixelRatio as ADVISORY (warn, don't block — anti-aliasing noise must not block deploys; DOM+number assertions are the blocking layer). Everything runs headless in the existing verify workflow topology; playwright-core is already vendored in verify/node_modules.
proposals.migration: Order (each step lands only with the gate green): (1) FREEZE: finish and baseline the acceptance suite against the current zero-build site; record all numeric goldens; tag the commit. (2) DATA OUT: mechanically extract the six data constants to src/data/*.json with the equivalence-proof script; re-baseline golden hash + DR; current dashboard.jsx temporarily reads the JSON via a tiny fetch-less inline include is NOT attempted — instead old files stay untouched and frozen while the new tree grows in parallel (no half-migrated serving state ever deploys). (3) MATH OUT: port src/lib modules expression-for-expression; unit tests must reproduce the golden fixtures exactly; wire mutation.js to them. (4) SHELL+ISLAND: scaffold Astro, port tabs one at a time into src/components (styles verbatim), mounting in the single Dashboard island; run the acceptance suite against `astro preview` after each tab and diff screenshots against the frozen baselines. (5) BUBBLEGAUGE: port as the lazy island with the typed interface; acceptance runs the full gating matrix incl. the never-fetched-chunk network assertion. (6) CONTROLS: retarget verify/ tests + calibration seeds; extend gate self-test; regenerate SBOM/provenance for dist. (7) CUTOVER: DRs amending CLAUDE.md (zero-build clause) + constitution digest; switch Pages to Actions deploy with gate-blocking + attestation in ONE commit; the old index.html/dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx are deleted in the same commit (Pages now serves only dist/, so no stale-file ambiguity). (8) CLOSE-OUT: file evidence closing R-GATE/B-01, update engagement-status via the sanctioned process, 48h canary watching the live site against acceptance run on the production URL (read-only).

SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH from commit time; exact-pinned toolchain via lockfile + Node .nvmrc; deterministic Rollup chunk naming (manualChunks + fixed hashing salt); provenance manifest generated from the SAME artifact that is uploaded to Pages, never from a separate rebuild"}, {"risk": "Perpetual toolchain upkeep under no-human-review — Astro/Vite major releases, npm advisories, and Playwright browser churn create a standing maintenance tax the agents must keep green forever; proposals.tradeoffs: Honest weighing of the content-first prior: this page is NOT content-heavy — of ~3,000 source lines, only the Playbook tab is genuinely prose-shaped; the rest is a stateful chart application. So Astro's headline features (MDX, content collections) are deliberately unused, and its real payoff here is narrower: (a) an enforced, framework-level island boundary that makes bubblegauge's zero-footprint contract a build artifact fact (the chunk is never fetched without the param) instead of a code-discipline promise; (b) a static pre-rendered shell + Playbook HTML giving fast first paint and a working noscript story, versus a blank div in a plain SPA; (c) zero-config Vite+TS with one integration to pin. The candid alternative is plain Vite+React SPA — roughly 90% of the benefit with one fewer framework layer and a smaller upgrade treadmill; I still pick Astro because the island boundary and static shell are load-bearing for two evaluation criteria (governance-visible egress/no-op guarantees, and first-paint UX), but if the panel weights simplicity harder, this design degrades gracefully to Vite-only by replacing src/pages/index.astro with index.html + main.tsx while keeping every other decision (data-as-JSON, lib modules, controls mapping, Actions deploy, testing) intact. Other accepted costs: the shipped supply chain grows from 5 audited CDN files to a lockfile-full of transitive packages (mitigated by npm ci integrity, SBOM, ratchet, and the fact that unpkg — a live third-party runtime dependency — disappears entirely, which is a net egress/availability win); the 119-check audit apparatus needs a real retargeting pass (~half the checks touch paths), which is scheduled, evidence-tracked work rather than a risk; and browsers now get ~200–300 KB gzip of same-origin JS instead of ~3 MB Babel plus per-load transpilation of 3,090 JSX lines — the single largest UX improvement available to this project.

proposals.name: Same Runtime, Compiled Ahead — esbuild-in-the-gate incremental hardening
proposals.summary: Keep the runtime conceptually identical (React 18.3.1 + Recharts 2.12.7 UMD globals, one page, inline styles) but move transpilation from the browser to CI: a ~80-line pinned esbuild script compiles the app from small ES modules into two hashed, SRI'd bundles (app + lazy bubblegauge chunk), vendors are self-hosted byte-identical UMD files (unpkg egress removed entirely), and Pages switches to deploy-from-Actions so the existing 119-check gate becomes literally deploy-blocking (structurally closing R-GATE/C-26/B-01). Crisis data is extracted to JSON with the golden hash provably unchanged; pure math becomes typed, mutation-tested modules. The decisive argument for LESS change: (1) the verify harness already transpiles dashboard.jsx with esbuild for every oracle, so shipping esbuild output aligns production with what is already tested, and (2) every migration step is a single attributable delta gated by a frozen black-box Playwright suite, whereas a full rewrite (Vite/React19/Recharts3 or hand-rolled SVG) puts the deepest-risk surface — Recharts fan charts, custom tooltips, seeded mulberry32 numbers — through an uncharacterized re-render with hundreds of new transitive deps to pin, for benefits (tree-shaking ~100KB, nicer DX) that score far below the parity and supply-chain risk they buy.
proposals.stack: {"framework": "React 18.3.1 + ReactDOM 18.3.1 + prop-types 15.8.1 + Recharts 2.12.7 — all kept as the EXACT self-hosted UMD production files (byte-identical to today's unpkg files, same SRI hashes re-verified), loaded as window globals; app code keeps referencing the globals so vendor code paths are provably unchanged", "language": "ES modules; incremental TypeScript — extracted pure math and data schemas are .ts from day one (esbuild type-strip only, zero runtime semantics change), components stay .jsx verbatim in phase 1 and convert to .tsx opportunistically; typescript@5.8.x runs tsc --noEmit as a gate check (checkJs for the not-yet-converted files via JSDoc); a local globals.d.ts types window.React/window.Recharts using @types/react + recharts's own types (typecheck-only deps, never bundled)", "build": "esbuild@0.25.5 exact-pinned (already the harness's transpiler), driven by a ~80-line build.mjs: two entries (src/main.jsx, src/bubblegauge/index.jsx as a dynamic-import chunk), format=esm, target=es2020, minify, sourcemap=external, entryNames=[name].[hash], deterministic (no timestamps, sorted inputs); build.mjs also stamps SRI integrity attributes for ALL scripts (vendors + own bundles — SRI works same-origin) into dist/index.html and regenerates governance/provenance-manifest.json; CI builds twice and byte-diffs to attest reproducibility. No Vite/webpack: one binary dep vs hundreds of transitive rollup/plugin deps the agent must keep pinned forever", "charts": "recharts@2.12.7 UMD kept, no upgrade, no replacement, NOT re-bundled from npm — re-bundling would swap the exact vendor bytes exercised by every existing chart (fan charts, matrix heatmap, custom tooltips) for a different module graph; hand-rolled SVG is a full re-characterization of the highest-risk surface and is rejected", "styling": "inline styles kept verbatim (they are part of pixel parity); no CSS framework", "data": "src/data/{crises,matrix,classification,taxonomy}.json + generated .d.ts — data-as-data, imported via esbuild JSON loader; the golden sha256 is computed over the identical payload object so the recorded hash 5c0c3a20… carries over unchanged, proving lossless extraction", "bubblegauge_boundary": "own directory src/bubblegauge/, compiled to a separate hashed chunk; a ~20-line inline gate in index.html (or main.jsx top) replicates today's KEY_RE/?status-api-off/sessionStorage/localhost-mapping logic and only then dynamic-import()s the chunk — zero-footprint becomes structural (without the param the chunk is never even fetched); window.BubbleGauge bridge replaced by a typed module interface with the same contract, INTEGRATION_NOTES.md contract unchanged for the viewer"}
proposals.structure: Repo: src/ (main.jsx entry; data/*.json + types; lib/math.ts [interp, rebase, logPath, mulberry32, runFan, buildAggregate, subFamily], lib/stats.ts [zArr, corrArr, xcorrRow], lib/format.ts; components/ ~12 files: AppShell.jsx, tabs/{CrisisExplorer,SimilarityMatrix,Aggregate,Analytics,Playbook}.jsx, charts/{FanChart,MatrixGrid,Tooltips}.jsx; bubblegauge/ ~5 files: index.jsx, gauge.jsx, feed.ts [status-API client + fixtures], gating.ts) — ~28 small modules total, none over ~300 lines. index.html stays a hand-written template (error overlay, noscript, loading div preserved verbatim); build.mjs injects hashed script tags + SRI. vendor/ holds the 3 UMD files (react, react-dom+prop-types, recharts) committed to the repo with their recorded hashes (Babel standalone deleted — the ~3MB download and per-load transpile disappear). dist/ is the built site (gitignored, produced only by CI). verify/, governance/, audit/, acceptance/ stay where they are; verify/lib/load.js simplifies (imports src/lib and src/data directly — no more stub-global evaluation of a 1876-line monolith, though the seeded-copy BG_REPO mechanism is kept for calibration). All URLs remain relative; site still works at any base path.
proposals.deploy: GitHub Pages 'Deploy from GitHub Actions'. One workflow, all actions pinned by full commit SHA: checkout → setup-node (Node 22 LTS, npm ci against committed package-lock.json) → verify gate (cd verify && node run.js && node mutation.js && node gate.js) → tsc --noEmit → node build.mjs (twice, byte-diff for reproducibility) → frozen Playwright acceptance suite against dist/ served locally → actions/attest-build-provenance@v2 over the Pages artifact (signed SLSA provenance: source commit → workflow → artifact digest, the buildable upgrade to residual R-LEDGER) → upload-pages-artifact/deploy-pages. Branch-push deploy is disabled, so the gate is DEPLOY-BLOCKING by construction — this is the structural close of R-GATE, C-26 and B-01 that the current non-enforcing ruleset cannot give. CNAME privacy rule unchanged (domain only in CNAME, copied into the artifact). Break-glass: re-enabling branch deploy requires a decision record. CLAUDE.md's zero-build clause is amended by DR before any code moves (owner has opened that door; Plan B clause satisfied).

update rule (decision record required) unchanged", "data_invariants_10": "unchanged assertions, now plain imports of src/lib + src/data (stub-global loader no longer needed for math)", "static_security_30": "runs over src/** AND dist/** output; adds: no eval/new Function in bundles, no non-allowlisted URL literals in built output, hashed filenames referenced only from index.html", "supply_chain_pins_35": "pinned-deps.json splits into (a) vendor manifest: the 3 committed UMD files hash-pinned to the SAME SRI values recorded today, plus an online CI check that re-downloads from unpkg and byte-compares (vendor bytes provably identical to what ships now); (b) package-lock.json: npm ci only, exact-pinned devDeps (esbuild, typescript, playwright-core, @types/react), a test enforces a transitive-dependency budget and no install scripts (esbuild's binary fetched via its own postinstall is pinned by lockfile integrity hash)", "sri_40": "STRONGER: integrity= on every script tag including own bundles; build.mjs regenerates governance/provenance-manifest.json per build with each output's sha256, the source files + hashes + esbuild version it derives_from, test evidence and owning_role carried per source module; layered under the signed Actions attestation (R-LEDGER upgrade)", "egress_allowlist_62": "allowlist SHRINKS: unpkg removed (self-hosted vendors), leaving only the gated status-API host and etoro outbound anchors; test scans src AND built bundles; browser still never calls CNN — feargreed contract test 63 unchanged", "mutation": "mutation.js retargets src/lib/math.ts and stats.ts directly (no in-memory monolith transpile); catch-rate re-measured post-migration and recorded in audit/02-calibration — this is the explicit safety net against the verify-suite rework silently weakening the net", "gate_failclosed": "gate.js findings-integrity + production-eligibility logic unchanged; migration closes R-GATE (Actions-only deploy) and materially advances C-26 (SBOM now = lockfile + vendor manifest + signed attestation), each closure via decision record, never by editing findings"}
proposals.testing: Unit: keep the zero-dependency deterministic verify runner (run.js) — no vitest/jest; the gate self-test and 'no stray *.test.js' control stay valid, dependency count stays minimal; pure-math tests get denser cases now that lib/ is importable. Acceptance (FROZEN before any migration step, against the current Babel site served by python3 -m http.server): Playwright (playwright-core already a devDep, pin 1.54.x) black-box suite in acceptance/tests — per-tab DOM text and number assertions (rendered fan-chart medians, matrix cell values pulled into acceptance/golden/computed-*.json which already exist), SVG structure sampling (path counts, sampled path coordinates within tolerance) for the Recharts surfaces, full-page screenshot diffs per tab with a small pixel tolerance, and the complete ?status-api contract: KEY_RE accept/reject, demo fixture mode fully offline, sessionStorage persistence across reload, ?status-api-off, localhost mapping, and — via Playwright route interception — the zero-footprint assertion that WITHOUT the param no request is made to the bubblegauge chunk or any API host. The same frozen suite runs unchanged against dist/ at every migration step and in the deploy workflow forever.
proposals.migration: Seven gated steps, each one PR-sized, gate + frozen acceptance green before the next: (1) finish and FREEZE the acceptance suite against the current production behavior; record goldens/screenshots. (2) Decision record amending CLAUDE.md (zero-build → built-by-the-gate) and DR-005-compatible workflow; no code change. (3) Switch Pages to deploy-from-Actions serving the UNCHANGED site (workflow just uploads the repo files) — isolates deploy-mechanism risk and closes R-GATE first, before any code moves. (4) Kill in-browser Babel: esbuild transpile-only (no bundling, no renaming) of the two byte-identical .jsx sources to two .js files; self-host the 4→3 vendor UMDs; delete the Babel script tag — the single biggest perf win (~3MB + per-load transpile gone) with zero source-code change, so any acceptance diff is attributable purely to the transpiler swap the harness already vouches for. (5) Extract data to src/data/*.json with the golden-hash-preservation proof; extract pure math to src/lib/*.ts; retarget mutation + invariants. (6) Modularize components mechanically (no reordering of side-effectful top-level code) and move bubblegauge behind the dynamic-import gate; switch to hashed bundles + own-bundle SRI. (7) tsc --noEmit into the gate; incremental .tsx conversion; re-run the 9-seed calibration and re-attest provenance/SBOM; update engagement-status blockers via decision records.

step 4 changes ONLY the transpiler with sources byte-identical, so the frozen acceptance suite (DOM numbers + screenshots) isolates any diff to that one cause; mulberry32/runFan outputs are golden-pinned to 4 decimal places"}, {"risk": "modularization drift: the two monoliths have load-order coupling (window.BubbleGauge defined before dashboard reads it) and top-level evaluation order that mechanical splitting could reorder", "mitigation": "extraction rules forbid reordering side-effectful top-level code; the bridge is replaced by an explicit import whose contract is asserted by acceptance tests (strip/6th-tab/re-anchoring present with key, absent without); computed-fans/aggregate golden JSONs compare end-to-end numbers after every extraction commit"}, {"risk": "the bundler breaks the bubblegauge zero-footprint contract (eager chunk fetch, preload hints, shared-chunk hoisting)", "mitigation": "bubblegauge is a separate esbuild entry loaded only by an explicit runtime dynamic import behind the KEY_RE gate; build.mjs emits no modulepreload hints for it; Playwright route interception asserts zero requests to the chunk and to any API host when the param is absent — this control runs on every deploy"}, {"risk": "reworking the ~7 path/regex-coupled verify tests and two manifests silently weakens the 119-check net (vacuous-green)", "mitigation": "the existing gate self-test, mutation floor, findings-file integrity, and above all the 9-seed calibration corpus re-run (BG_REPO seeded copies must still be CAUGHT after retargeting) make suite-weakening detectable; test retargeting lands in the same commit as each layout change, never batched"}, {"risk": "CI becomes the new supply-chain/availability surface: compromised action, lockfile drift, esbuild binary provenance, or a red pipeline blocking all deploys forever", "mitigation": "all actions pinned by full commit SHA; npm ci with committed lockfile + integrity hashes; dependency-budget and no-new-install-scripts tests; double-build byte-diff proves reproducibility; signed artifact attestation ties artifact→commit→workflow; break-glass branch-deploy re-enable documented as a decision-record-gated escape hatch"}]
proposals.tradeoffs: Honest concessions of the minimal path: (1) keeping vendors as UMD globals forgoes tree-shaking — total JS served is ~450KB gzip vendors + ~60KB app vs maybe ~350KB for a bundled-from-npm build; I accept ~100KB to keep vendor bytes provably identical (and note the REAL perf problem — 3MB Babel + per-load transpile of 3000 lines — is eliminated either way; first paint improves by seconds, not the marginal 100KB). (2) TypeScript is incremental, not total: window-global typing for Recharts is a .d.ts shim, weaker than native ESM imports; the pure-math layer (where correctness lives and mutation bites) is fully typed first, components follow. (3) The 119-check apparatus needs moderate, unavoidable rework (~7 test files, 2 manifests, mutation retarget, calibration re-run) — no architecture avoids this, but this one keeps every control's SEMANTICS identical so the rework is retargeting, not redesign. (4) No framework/router/SSR conveniences — correct for a single-page read-only dashboard; adding them is pure risk here. Where a full rewrite would genuinely win — Recharts 2.12.7 will age (React 19 incompat someday), and hand-rolled SVG would remove a 500KB dep — I judge that a future, separately-gated decision the module structure makes CHEAPER later (charts are isolated in src/components/charts/), not something to couple to this migration while parity risk carries a 25% weight and the frozen acceptance suite is the only reviewer.

judges.lens_verdict: Under a parity-and-migration-safety lens, the decisive questions are: (a) do the exact vendor bytes that render today's pixels keep rendering them, (b) is every migration delta singly attributable and bisectable, and (c) does the plan preserve rather than re-baseline the existing evidentiary anchors (golden hash 5c0c3a20…, mutation literals, calibration seeds). Repo verification sharpened the ranking: verify/lib/load.js ALREADY runs all data/math oracles through esbuild transformSync, mutation.js hard-fails on not-applied literal mutants, the golden hash is provably carryable through lossless JSON extraction, and acceptance/tests/ is empty (the frozen suite is unbuilt for everyone). Proposal 4 wins because its riskiest step — swapping in-browser Babel for CI esbuild — is executed on byte-identical sources with byte-identical self-hosted vendor UMDs, i.e. the one transformation the standing harness has been characterizing all along, and its ordering (freeze suite → flip deploy with unchanged code → transpile-only → extract data with hash-identity proof → modularize) is the most bisectable path offered; its costs (incremental TS, unminified Recharts, globals-shim typing) are maintainability and perf costs, not parity costs. Proposal 1 is a close second: the best end-state for agent maintainability and the best bundle, but it trades away vendor-byte identity for version identity on the single deepest risk surface (recharts fan charts/tooltips) and understates both its transitive-dependency surface and the mutation/test-35 retargeting coupling. Proposal 2 correctly maximizes vendor parity but then spends the entire saved risk budget rewriting all 3,090 render lines into htm — the largest hand-transform on the table, in the dialect agents know least — which is the wrong trade under this lens. Proposal 3 ranks last despite the best first-paint story: it stacks the ESM-recharts drift risk with a second framework layer, structurally changes the Playbook DOM in a way its own frozen-suite commitment cannot absorb, floats its Vite pin, and — the clearest verified error — elects a golden-hash re-baseline where hash identity was available, weakening the exact control that makes agent-only maintenance of frozen data trustworthy.

judges.lens_verdict: Through the agent-maintainability-and-governance lens, the decisive facts are repo-verified: the verify harness ALREADY runs every oracle through esbuild transformSync, the mutation operators are literal substrings whose semantics survive verbatim ports, and the golden hash provably carries over under faithful JSON extraction. That makes Proposal 4 (esbuild-in-the-gate) the winner: it changes the fewest things the 119-check apparatus is coupled to, keeps every control's SEMANTICS identical (retargeting, not redesign), keeps SRI meaningful on same-origin bundles instead of retiring it, adds exactly one build dependency, and sequences the migration so the deploy flip (closing R-GATE first, before any code moves) and the transpiler swap are each single attributable deltas — the safest shape for a repo whose only reviewer is its test suite. Proposal 1 (Vite+TS) is a very close second and is arguably better for the 5-year maintainability horizon (real ESM imports, strict TS, idiomatic tooling agents know cold); it loses narrowly because its permanent dev-dep/config treadmill is the largest and its recharts-ESM re-bundling is the one parity unknown Proposal 4 structurally avoids — but it is the natural 'phase 2' that P4's module structure makes cheap later. Proposal 2's governance story is the purest (artifact == commit bytes, reproducibility by construction) but it spends the project's scarcest resource — agent fluency — on htm, a niche dialect every future no-human-review edit must be written in; that is exactly backwards for this regime, and its CSP claim doesn't survive contact with the inline scripts it needs. Proposal 3 is the honest loser: it argues convincingly against its own framework choice, its static-shell Playbook idea creates a frozen-content duplication hazard, it moves the KEY_RE gate into an inline script outside the typed/tested tree, and it needlessly re-baselines the golden hash — the one ritual this governance regime exists to make painful.

judges.lens_verdict: Through a pragmatics-performance-simplicity lens, D (esbuild-in-the-gate) wins because it is the only proposal grounded in what the repo actually is: it correctly quotes the golden hash, the mutation oracle values, and the fact that verify/ already runs esbuild and already carries playwright-core — and it kills the one real performance problem (3MB Babel + per-load transpile of 3,090 lines) while holding vendor bytes and source bytes constant through single-cause, PR-sized deltas, which is what a frozen black-box suite can actually adjudicate. Its perf ceiling (no tree-shaking, unmeasured ~450KB-gz vendor claim) and open-ended incremental-TS endpoint are the honest costs. A is the best long-run agent-maintainability play (real TS strict, toolchain agents know best) and the best performer, but it spends its parity budget on the exact surface everyone agrees is riskiest — re-bundling recharts from npm instead of shipping the UMD bytes the pixels were rendered by — and undercounts its lockfile-treadmill cost; a strong second, and the sensible phase-2 destination D's module structure makes cheap later. B is the governance purist's option (artifact==commit, zero shipped npm deps, offline SRI, CSP — the best supply-chain story here) but pays for it with the worst payload of the four and a permanent htm dialect tax on the very agents who must maintain it unreviewed — it optimizes the 20% criterion at the expense of both 25% criteria. C eliminates itself: it concedes Vite-only yields 90% of its benefit, its static-Playbook headline conflicts with Playbook being a tab inside the client:only island, and it carries the largest upgrade treadmill in a repo whose entire regime is pin-and-freeze. Practical recommendation: adopt D's migration (notably its step ordering — freeze acceptance, flip Pages-to-Actions BEFORE any code moves, kill Babel with byte-identical sources), and treat A's Vite/full-TS endpoint as a separately gated future DR once the frozen suite has proven itself as the reviewer.

double-build byte-diff on every deploy; signed SLSA attestation binding artifact to commit and workflow; DR-gated break-glass to re-enable branch deploy documented before it is ever needed; rollback = re-run deploy from a prior green commit."}, {"risk": "Slow-motion relitigating under no-human-review: over years, agents 'helpfully' bump esbuild/playwright, swap vendor UMDs for npm ESM, upgrade Recharts, or loosen the CSP/allowlist to fix a red CI — eroding the pins the whole regime rests on.", "mitigation": "Every pin is enforced by a gate check with a calibration seed, not by documentation alone (vendor-pins offline hash recompute, exact-version lint, dependency budget, no-.jsx ratchet, egress flat-zero ratchets); CLAUDE.md's amended architecture section states the load-bearing impossibilities explicitly (no Recharts 3.x/React 19 without a DR; vendor bytes are hash-frozen); the ONLY sanctioned evolution path — the Vite phase-2 shim swap — is pre-documented so future improvement pressure has a legitimate outlet instead of an ad-hoc one."}]
synthesis.one_paragraph_pitch: Adopt the consensus winner's chassis with the runner-up's destination: move transpilation from every viewer's browser into the CI gate using the exact esbuild version (0.19.12) the verify harness has been characterizing all along, keep react-dom 18.3.1 and recharts 2.12.7 as byte-identical self-hosted UMD files (synthesizer-measured at just ~170KB gzip total, with sha384s already proven equal to today's SRI hashes — so chart parity is guaranteed by identity, not hoped for), and land at a fully typed, ~35-module TypeScript codebase whose frozen data lives as canonical JSON carrying the existing golden hash 5c0c3a20… forward UNCHANGED. The migration is a strictly ordered sequence of single-cause, bisectable deltas adjudicated by a Playwright acceptance suite frozen against the current site before anything moves — and the deploy flip to gate-blocked GitHub Actions happens FIRST, while the code is still untouched, structurally closing R-GATE/C-26/B-01 (a red gate physically cannot publish) and adding signed SLSA provenance over the served artifact. Every one of the 119-check apparatus's controls maps forward with its semantics intact and must re-catch its calibration seed before its retarget counts, the mutation harness keeps its 13 mutants and its hard-fail tripwire, unpkg egress disappears entirely, viewers stop downloading 661KB gzip of Babel and stop paying 1-3 seconds of transpile CPU per load, and the ?status-api contract survives clause-for-clause with its gating logic finally a pure, table-tested function and its optional chunk never even fetched without the key. Every flaw the three judges named is resolved in writing — including two facts none of them had: the vendor-size fear that drove half the debate was wrong by 40x, and the gate's required-check set couples to test filenames, which this plan freezes.
